title: Uragoga podocephala
taxonomy: Uragoga podocephala is classified as a species of plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Uragoga. Uragoga podocephala is native to tropical Africa. The plant is further classified as a perennial herb. More research is needed to fully understand the taxonomic classification of this species.

intro: Uragoga podocephala, also known as Podo or Uragoga, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Uragoga podocephala include its potential to treat various conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, and kidney stones, due to its diuretic, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga podocephala include iridoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Uragoga podocephala is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
